subject: SIP Weekly Update - Week ending 26 Sep
deck_filename: SIP weekly update - week ending 26 Sep_vSent.pdf

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

Please find SIP weekly update attached for your review.

Thank you.
Cuiwei

content.question:
Thanks



Slide 4

   - Can we have a total view?
- Does this reflects the new charging model?

Slide 5

   - MYSG dropped so much?
Who is the owner of direct selling?

MY, SG or
   SIP team?
Who are the RMs managing these sellers now?

Slide 6 and 9

   - When will we use the new chatbot?

Can remind me why we need to wait
   for rollout?

Slide 16

   - For Dino, how are we approaching CN sellers who are not part of CN SIP
   to be part of this?

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

Slide 4

   -

   Can we have a total view?
   -

      Added total combined P&L (standalone+MP+SLS P&L) view for local SIP
      as below. Total combined P&L EOM proj $3.0M, -$0.5M MoM driven by MP PC2
      decline
      -

         SG/TH: CIR% +1.7pp MoM to all CB sellers
         -

         MY: CIR% +2.9pp MoM for SIP fashion push

[image: image.png]

   -

   CNSIP pending data fix will start to show from this week.



   -

   Does this reflects the new charging model?
   -

      Yes, we have reflected the big seller discount to seller/standalone
      P&L.




Slide 5

   -

   MYSG dropped so much? Who is the owner of direct selling? MY, SG or SIP
   team? Who are the RMs managing these sellers now?
   -

      MYSG topline drop in Sep vs Aug was mainly due to SGCB traffic
      boosting issue in early Aug leading to higher than expected ADO
in Aug. Sep
      vs Aug drop is similar to the overall SGCB Sep vs Aug trend. If comparing
      Sep vs Jul, ADO as %CB +0.5pp, ADG as %CB +0.7pp.
      -

      On ownership, overall direct selling business owner is each export
      market; role split as below:
      -

         Export market: acquire and support sellers in overseas selling
         (drive price competitiveness, marketing investment, campaign
participation,
         etc.)
         -

         Import market: treat direct sellers the same way as CN/KR/JP CB MP
         sellers
         -

         Reg SIP: PMO direct business - setup inter-market processes,
         establish system/ infrastructure/ workflows, design target and drive
         regional initiatives to accelerate growth across markets
         -

      For MYSG, currently in transition to MY export team based on above
      principle from mid-Oct onwards
      -

         [Current] MYSG Direct team (under SGBD) owns seller acquisition
         and seller management for MYSG lane
         -

content.question:
Slide 16

   - For Dino, how are we approaching CN sellers who are not part of CN SIP
   to be part of this?

- UST sellers: CNSIP mass pitching (opt-out model) + 1:1 RM pitching.
      26 UST sellers (33%) are now with Dino shop.
      - Non-UST sellers: CNSIP mass pitching (opt-out model) only
      - Total 54K GPs pitched for Dino including 8.7k new to CNSIP, overall
      92% success rate (summary table below)



*Dino pitching*

*Pitched GP#*

*Accepted GP#*

*Acceptance rate*

Existing CNSIP

49,961

45,894

92%

New to CNSIP-Dino

8,703

7,599

87%

*Total*

53,956

49,390

92%



How about adjacent markets push?
How would it work?

*From:* Cuiwei Sun <cuiwei.sun@shopee.com>
*Sent:* Thursday, 2 October 2025 1:52 pm
*To:* Ian Ho <hoi@sea.com>
*Cc:* Jianghong Liu <jianghong.liu@shopee.com>; Shuning Wang <
shuning.wang@shopee.com>; Keljin Chua <keljin.chua@shopee.com>; Jason Yu <
jason.yu@shopee.com>; Yu Zhang <yu.zhang@shopee.com>; Ailin Xie <
ailin.xie@shopee.com>; Wei Qiang <wei.qiang@shopee.com>; Huang Wan-Jung <
wanjung.huangwj@shopee.com>; Chiong Zhi Ann <zhiann.chiong@shopee.com>; Wei
Loong Yong <weiloong.yong@shopee.com>; Linda Sun <linda.sun@shopee.com>;
Xiao Xuan Soh <xiaoxuan.soh@shopee.com>; Keith Huang <keith.huang@shopee.com>;
Joe Li <joe.liyc@shopee.com>; Lim Wan Ting <wanting.lim@shopee.com>; Ren
Jiahui <jiahui.ren@shopee.com>; Eveline Seah <eveline.seahyl@shopee.com>;
Lee Jia Yi <jiayi.jylee@shopee.com>; Faye Yan <faye.yan@shopee.com>
*Subject:* Re: SIP Weekly Update - Week ending 26 Sep



Hi Ian,



Slide 4

   - Can we have a total view?

- Added total combined P&L (standalone+MP+SLS P&L) view for local SIP as
      below. Total combined P&L EOM proj $3.0M, -$0.5M MoM driven by MP PC2
      decline


   - SG/TH: CIR% +1.7pp MoM to all CB sellers
         - MY: CIR% +2.9pp MoM for SIP fashion push

o   CNSIP pending data fix will start to show from this week.



   - Does this reflects the new charging model?

- Yes, we have reflected the big seller discount to seller/standalone
      P&L.





Slide 5

   - MYSG dropped so much?
Who is the owner of direct selling?

MY, SG or
   SIP team?
Who are the RMs managing these sellers now?

- MYSG topline drop in Sep vs Aug was mainly due to SGCB traffic
      boosting issue in early Aug leading to higher than expected ADO
in Aug. Sep
      vs Aug drop is similar to the overall SGCB Sep vs Aug trend. If comparing
      Sep vs Jul, ADO as %CB +0.5pp, ADG as %CB +0.7pp.
      - On ownership, overall direct selling business owner is each export
      market; role split as below:


   - Export market: acquire and support sellers in overseas selling (drive
         price competitiveness, marketing investment, campaign
participation, etc.)
         - Import market: treat direct sellers the same way as CN/KR/JP CB
         MP sellers
         - Reg SIP: PMO direct business - setup inter-market processes,
         establish system/ infrastructure/ workflows, design target and drive
         regional initiatives to accelerate growth across markets


   - For MYSG, currently in transition to MY export team based on above
      principle from mid-Oct onwards


   - [Current] MYSG Direct team (under SGBD) owns seller acquisition and
         seller management for MYSG lane
         - [To-be] MY Export team (under MYCB) owns MY seller acquisition
         and seller management for all export (sip+direct) lanes (MYSG/PH/TH)



Slide 6 and 9

   - When will we use the new chatbot?

Can remind me why we need to wait
   for rollout?

- Pls refer to below table on the rollout timeline

*SIP Buyer Market*

*ETA for agent migration to PH*

*ETA for new chatbot rollout*

*ID-SG/MY/PH*

N/A

Oct

*PH*

N/A

Completed in Sep

*TW*

Completed in Aug

Completed in Sep

*SG*

Completed in Sep

Completed in Sep

*TH*

Completed in Sep

Oct

*MY*

Oct

Oct

*VN*

Nov

Nov



o   We start new chatbot rollout alongside agent migration to PH, as new
chatbot has lower deflection rate than old one, that requires more agents
to handle additional chats. We should hire the additional agents needed
directly in PH for the long-term, instead of hiring temporarily in existing
markets; thus the dependency on PH agent migration.



Slide 16

   - For Dino, how are we approaching CN sellers who are not part of CN SIP
   to be part of this?

content.answer:
Hi Ian,


How about adjacent markets push? How would it work?


Adjacent market launch will follow the same approach as Dino



Acquisition owner

Acquisition approach

VN-KH SIP, TH-MM/LA SIP

VN/TH BD team

   -

   mass pitching + RM 1:1 pitching for top local sellers who are not on
   VN/TH local SIP
   -

   mass pitching to other sellers

CNSIP

CNCB SMT team

   -

   mass pitching + RM 1:1 pitching for UST sellers who are not on CNSIP
   -

   mass pitching for non-UST sellers

Other local SIP lanes

Reg SIP team

   -

   mass pitching to all sellers (existing SIP acquisition approach)

content.question: Am referring to CN side, is there a way to pitch for all markets at one go?
Instead of 1 by 1?

*From:* Cuiwei Sun <cuiwei.sun@shopee.com>
*Sent:* Tuesday, 7 October 2025 10:44 am
*To:* Ian Ho <hoi@sea.com>
*Cc:* Jianghong Liu <jianghong.liu@shopee.com>; Shuning Wang <
shuning.wang@shopee.com>; Keljin Chua <keljin.chua@shopee.com>; Jason Yu <
jason.yu@shopee.com>; Yu Zhang <yu.zhang@shopee.com>; Ailin Xie <
ailin.xie@shopee.com>; Wei Qiang <wei.qiang@shopee.com>; Huang Wan-Jung <
wanjung.huangwj@shopee.com>; Chiong Zhi Ann <zhiann.chiong@shopee.com>; Wei
Loong Yong <weiloong.yong@shopee.com>; Linda Sun <linda.sun@shopee.com>;
Xiao Xuan Soh <xiaoxuan.soh@shopee.com>; Keith Huang <keith.huang@shopee.com>;
Joe Li <joe.liyc@shopee.com>; Lim Wan Ting <wanting.lim@shopee.com>; Ren
Jiahui <jiahui.ren@shopee.com>; Eveline Seah <eveline.seahyl@shopee.com>;
Lee Jia Yi <jiayi.jylee@shopee.com>; Faye Yan <faye.yan@shopee.com>
*Subject:* Re: SIP Weekly Update - Week ending 26 Sep



Hi Ian,



How about adjacent markets push?
How would it work?

content.answer:
Got it. Per HQ/CN legal guidance, we can pitch for all markets at one go if
the confirmed launch dates are same/close. We just need to state the market
names clearly in the pitching message, and give sellers options to choose
which ones they want to opt-out.

Got it. Per HQ/CN legal guidance, we can pitch for all markets at one go if
the confirmed launch dates are same/close. We just need to state the market
names clearly in the pitching message, and give sellers options to choose
which ones they want to opt-out.

names clearly in the pitching message, and give sellers options to choose
which ones they want to opt-out.

Got it. Per HQ/CN legal guidance, we can pitch for all markets at one go if
the confirmed launch dates are same/close. We just need to state the market
names clearly in the pitching message, and give sellers options to choose
which ones they want to opt-out.
